corrective_reason: Run 001 changed ego_status[7], but SparseDrive's released nuScenes CAN-bus contract stores forward speed at ego_status[6] and InstanceQueue reads index 6 into its longitudinal ego anchor. Run 001's ego-speed sensitivity decision is rejected. Its output directory is retained.

coordinate_boundary.mapping: CAN [forward, left, up] = model [y, -x, z] for acceleration and velocity

purpose: Correctively test whether the pinned SparseDrive plan on the existing real source window causally depends on RGB, correctly ordered ego forward velocity, and ego-pose history. This does not test semantic correctness or simulator credibility.
held_fixed: source timestamps, calibration, projection matrices and navigation command except inside the declared intervention
SparseDrive source, compatibility patch, checkpoint and reset procedure
four-frame warm-up and native six-step planning output

rgb_spatial_information.control: replace every camera pixel with the rounded ImageNet normalization mean

rgb_temporal_history.control: repeat the first source RGB frame at all four logical timestamps

ego_pose_history.control: repeat the first model-to-world pose in SparseDrive img_metas across all four logical timestamps

primary_observation: fully warmed native six-point planning trajectory at source frame 30
repeat_reference: independent reset with identical RGB, corrected state, calibration, pose and command

decision_rules.rgb_stream_influence: accepted only if the constant-RGB warmed-plan maximum absolute coordinate change exceeds the paired repeat maximum plus 0.0001 m
decision_rules.visual_history_influence: accepted only if the frozen-RGB-history warmed-plan maximum absolute coordinate change exceeds the paired repeat maximum plus 0.0001 m
decision_rules.ego_velocity_influence: accepted only if both plus and minus 2.0 m/s warmed-plan changes exceed the paired repeat maximum plus 0.0001 m
decision_rules.ego_pose_history_influence: accepted only if the frozen-pose-history warmed-plan maximum absolute coordinate change exceeds the paired repeat maximum plus 0.0001 m
decision_rules.shortcut_or_semantic_correctness: not decidable from effect magnitude; report only as down-weighted or rejected overclaim

descriptive_diagnostics: fully warmed plan ADE and endpoint difference from corrected baseline
plan ADE to constant-velocity extrapolation formed from each supplied ego status
difference between the corrective baseline and the prior qualification baseline
strongest_allowed_claim: On this one real, near-straight, four-frame input slice, a declared RGB, correctly indexed ego-speed or ego-pose-history intervention did or did not causally change the frozen SparseDrive native plan beyond paired numerical repeat sensitivity.
forbidden_claims: RGB sensitivity proves correct object, lane or risk semantics
ego-pose sensitivity proves shortcut learning rather than necessary temporal alignment
a plausible plan under removed RGB proves a safe fallback
the larger effect from non-commensurate interventions proves feature dominance
the result qualifies HUGSIM or establishes SparseDrive safety
one slice rules out learned shortcuts in general
